channel.title: Forge
channel.description: Updates for Forge — agent-first development workbench from Coursion.

item.title: Version 0.9.1
item.description:
2026-07-19

  • Goals — Claude Code's native /goal, first-class. Type /goal in a Claude pane for a native panel of saved goal templates (one-tap Run) or a free-text verifiable end-state with an optional time budget. A goal bar tracks the run — elapsed, Stop, then met / not met — and .forge/goals.json + an inbox make goals agent-scriptable. Old Loops specs migrate to templates automatically; the Loops pane is retired.
  • Services pane — a process manager for dev servers. Long-lived processes (Metro, APIs, docker compose) with health checks, restart policies, and dependency-ordered Start all; agent-scriptable via .forge/services.json + inbox.
  • New workspaces open with your default agent — Claude Code out of the box, or pick any detected CLI (Settings → Agents).
  • Workspace status you can trust. Working dots survive workspace switches and long tool runs; a pane genuinely waiting on you shows an orange "waiting for input"; finished checkmarks clear themselves once seen (and expire if never visited); interrupts no longer fire phantom "finished" notifications.
  • Claude Code UI fixes. Transcript rows no longer vanish until you scroll; the idle 100% CPU spin is gone at the root; errors render as unmissable red alert cards; the worktree "Isolate" banner no longer hides the header row and its dismissal finally sticks.
  • Model picker, finished. "Default" now names the actual model ("Default — Opus 4.8 (1M)"), and your pick is remembered per workspace across restarts.
  • Sync fixes. BYO API keys actually roam via iCloud (both directions, stale copies cleaned); .forge/board.json refreshes after agents edit board.md directly.

item.title: Version 0.8.1
item.description:
2026-07-14

  • Claude Code UI mode — a native chat for Claude Code — new Claude panes open as a real UI over the same claude session (the terminal stays one toggle away): markdown with syntax-highlighted code, collapsible thinking, tool rows with live diffs and checklists, permission cards with one-tap "Always allow", answerable multiple-choice questions, / command autocomplete, @ file mentions, and image attachments. Resumed panes rehydrate their full transcript from disk.
  • Slash commands as real UI — /model, /effort, /config, /permissions, /mcp and /agents open native pickers and editable panels in headless mode, reading and writing Claude's on-disk config atomically.
  • Ultracode effort tier — the effort picker now includes the CLI's session-only ultracode mode (xhigh reasoning + multi-agent workflow orchestration); applied as --effort ultracode on the session, never saved to settings.json.
  • Skimmable transcripts — collapse any answer (or all at once) to a smart one-line summary with word and code-block counts, and jump to any prompt you've sent with the message navigator.
  • Active Agents in the sidebar (optional) — every pane currently working or waiting across all workspaces, with live status dots and click-to-jump.
  • Send Feedback + opt-in crash reporting — a feedback sheet with optional diagnostics, composed locally and sent through your mail client; unclean exits offer to send the crash report on next launch.
